When Empress Lu learned about it, she cried day and night and said to the emperor: I only have a prince and a daughter, how can I bear to abandon her to marry the Huns!
After all, the emperor could not send the eldest princess, so he found a palace maid to marry Lord Moton as his wife in the name of the eldest princess.
At the same time, Liu Jing was sent to conclude a peace and marriage alliance with the Xiongnu.
When Liu Jing came back from the Huns, he said that the two tribes of the Huns in Henan, Aiyang and Loufan, were only 700 miles away from Chang'an, and light cavalry could reach the Guanzhong area in one day and one night.
The Guanzhong area had just gone through the war and was still very depleted, with a sparse population, but the land was fertile and could be gradually enriched by immigrants.
When the princes from all over the country raised armies to attack, they would not have prospered if it had not been for the participation of the Tian clan of Qi State and the Zhao, Qu and Jing clans of Chu State.
Now, although His Majesty built the capital in Guanzhong, there is actually a lack of population.
The north is close to the Huns and the enemy, and the east is the old nobles of the six countries. The clan power is very strong. If anything happens, your majesty cannot rest easy.
I hope that your majesty will move the Tian clan of Qi State, the three major clans of Zhao, Qu and Jing of Chu State, the descendants of Yan, Zhao, Han, Wei and other countries, as well as the wealthy and famous people, to live in Guanzhong.
If there is peace in the country, you can guard against the Xiongnu; if there is trouble in the vassal states, you can also lead them eastward to attack.
This is a strategy to strengthen central power and weaken local power. The emperor said: Very good.
So Liu Jing was sent to move more than 100,000 people to Guanzhong according to his own suggestions.
Shu Suntong was from Xuexian County.
During the Qin Dynasty, he was recruited into the palace for being good at articles and knowledgeable, waiting to be appointed as a doctor.
A few years later, Chen Sheng raised troops in Shandong, and envoys reported the situation to the court. Qin II summoned doctors and Confucian scholars and asked: The soldiers guarding the border in Chu captured Qi County and entered Chen County. What do you think of this matter?
More than thirty doctors and Confucian scholars came forward and said, "The ministers cannot gather in crowds. Gathering in crowds is rebellion. This is a capital crime that cannot be pardoned. I hope your Majesty will send troops to attack them as soon as possible."
Qin II became angry when he heard this, and his expression suddenly changed.
At this time Shusun Tong walked forward and said: What all the Confucian scholars said is wrong.
Now that the world has become one, we have destroyed counties, cities, and cities, and sold all kinds of weapons to make it clear to the world that they will no longer use them.
What's more, when a wise monarch reigns over the world and formulates complete laws and regulations for those below, so that everyone obeys the law and obeys their duties, and everyone from all directions submits to the court, how can anyone dare to rebel?
This is just a group of thieves stealing, nothing to worry about. The county officials are hunting them down and punishing them, which is not worth worrying about.
Qin Er said happily: "Okay."
I asked every Confucian scholar again. Some Confucian scholars said they were rebels, and some said they were thieves.
So Qin II ordered the censors to examine what every Confucian scholar said, and anyone who said they were rebels would be handed over to officials for punishment. Qin II believed that they should not say such things.
Those who were said to be thieves were removed from their posts.
But he gave Shusun Tong twenty pieces of silk, a set of clothes, and the title of doctorate.
Shusun Tong walked out of the palace and returned to his residence. Some Confucian scholars asked: What flattering words did the master say?
Shusun Tong said: You don’t know, I can hardly escape from the tiger’s mouth!
So he fled the capital and arrived at Xue County. At that time, Xue County had surrendered to the Chu army.
When Xiang Liang arrived in Xue County, Shusun Tong took refuge with him.
Later, Xiang Liang died in battle in Dingtao, and Shusun Tong followed Xiong Xin, King Huai of Chu.
King Huai was conferred the title of Righteous Emperor by Xiang Yu and moved to Changsha. Shusun Tong stayed behind to serve Xiang Yu.
In the second year of Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty, Liu Bang, the king of Han, led five princes and kings to attack Pengcheng, and Shusun Tong surrendered to the king of Han.
The King of Han was defeated and went west, and Shusun Tong followed him and finally joined the King of Han.
Shusun Tong always wore the clothes of a Confucian scholar, which the King of Han disliked very much. So he changed his clothes and put on a short coat, which was made according to the custom of Chu. The King of Han was very happy to see him.
When Shusun Tong surrendered to the King of Han, he had more than a hundred Confucian disciples, but Shusun Tong never recommended them, but only praised and recommended those warriors who had gathered together to steal.
The Confucian disciples all secretly scolded him: "I served Mr. Feng for several years, and I was lucky enough to defect to the King of Han with him. Now I can't recommend us, but I praise a particularly cunning person. What's the point?"
When Shusun Tong heard the words scolding him, he said to the Confucian scholars: The king of Han is fighting for the world with sharp arrows and strong rocks. Can you Confucian scholars fight?
So I first want to praise the warriors who risk their lives to kill generals and seize flags.
Please wait for me, I won't forget you.
The king of Han appointed Shusun Tong as a doctor and called him the Sijun of Ji.
In the fifth year of Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty, the world had been unified. The princes in Dingtao jointly honored the King of Han as the emperor, and Shusun Tong was responsible for formulating the rituals and etiquette.
At that time, Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty abolished all the strict etiquette regulations of the Qin Dynasty and only formulated some simple and easy-to-implement rules.
However, the ministers were drinking and having fun in the court and arguing about merits. Some of them were shouting and screaming when they were drunk, and even drew their swords to chip away at the pillars of the court. Emperor Gao was troubled by this matter.
Shusun Tong knew that the emperor was becoming more and more disgusted with such things, so he advised: It is difficult for those Confucian scholars to attack and capture for you, but they can help you preserve the results.
I hope to recruit some Confucian scholars from Lu to work with my disciples to formulate court etiquette.
Emperor Gao said: I'm afraid it will be as cumbersome and difficult as in the past, right?
Shusun Tong said: The five emperors have different rituals, and the three kings have different rituals.
Ritual is to formulate rules of moderation or modification for people according to the world's affairs and customs at that time.
Therefore, we can understand this from the inheritance, deletion and addition of etiquette in the Xia, Yin and Zhou dynasties, which means that the etiquette in different dynasties is not repeated.
I am willing to use some ancient etiquette and combine it with the etiquette of the Qin Dynasty to formulate new etiquette.
The emperor said: You can try to do it, but make it easy to understand and consider what I can do.
So Shusun Tong was ordered to recruit more than thirty Confucian scholars from Lu.
There are two Confucian scholars in Lu who don't want to leave, saying: "You have served nearly ten monarchs, and they all gained closeness and authority through face-to-face flattery."
Now that the world has just been peaceful, the dead have not yet been buried, and the disabled have not yet recovered, yet regulations on rituals and music must be formulated.
Judging from the root cause of the establishment of rituals and music, it can only be popularized after a hundred years of accumulated merit.
We cannot do this for you against your will.
What you are doing is not according to ancient laws, so we will not do it.
Go ahead and don't insult us!
Shusun Tong smiled and said: You are really despicable Confucian scholars, you don't understand the changes of the times at all.
He then went west to the capital with the thirty men he had recruited. Together with the emperor's learned attendants and more than a hundred disciples of Shusun Tong, they pulled up ropes in the suburbs to show salutes and erected thatches to represent the dignity of the people. After practicing for more than a month, Shusuntong said: The emperor can come to inspect.
After the emperor inspected them, he asked them to salute him and then said: I can do this.
So he ordered all the ministers to come to study. It happened to be October, and the actual rehearsal for the New Year's Meeting could be carried out.
In the seventh year of Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty, the Changle Palace had been built. All the princes, kings and court officials came to pay homage to the emperor and participate in the New Year Ceremony.
The ceremony is: first at dawn, the visitor begins to preside over the ceremony and guides the princes, ministers, civil and military officials to enter the palace gate in sequence. Chariots, cavalry, infantry and palace guards are lined up in the court, various weapons are displayed, and various flags are erected.
The visitor called, "Go quickly with small steps."
So all the officials took their seats. Below the main hall, the doctors and officials stood on both sides of the steps. There were hundreds of people on the steps.
All the heroes, princes, generals and officers at all levels are arranged in order on the west side, facing east; all civil officials starting from the prime minister are arranged in order on the east side, facing west.
The nine protocol officers arranged by the Daxing Order called from top to bottom.
So the emperor came out of the palace in a dragon chariot. Hundreds of officials raised flags to call for warning, and then guided officials at all levels from the princes to more than six hundred stones to respectfully salute and congratulate the emperor.
All the officials below the princes and kings were not frightened and awed by this majestic ceremony.
Wait until the ceremony is over, and then arrange a banquet.
All the princes and officials sitting in the main hall held their breath and lowered their heads. They stood up in order of honor to congratulate and toast the emperor.
After nine rounds of pouring wine, the visitor announced that the banquet was over.
Finally, the supervisory officials enforced the etiquette regulations, identified those who did not comply with the etiquette regulations, and took them away.
From the court to the banquet, no one dared to speak loudly or act inappropriately.
After the ceremony, Emperor Gao said very proudly: Only today do I know the dignity of being an emperor.
So he gave Shusun Tong the official position of Taichang and rewarded him with five hundred catties of gold.
Shusun Tong took the opportunity to speak and said: My disciples, Confucian scholars, have been following me for a long time. They have worked with me to formulate court etiquette. I hope that your majesty will grant them official positions.
Emperor Gao made them all officials.
After Shusun Tong left the palace, he distributed five hundred kilograms of gold to various Confucian scholars.
These Confucian scholars all said happily: Mr. Shusun is really a great sage who knows the important affairs of the day.
In the ninth year of Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty, Emperor Gao appointed his uncle Sun Tong as the crown prince and tutor.
In the twelfth year of Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty, Emperor Gao planned to let Zhao Wang Liu Ruyi replace the prince. Shusun Tong advised the emperor: In the past, Duke Xiang of Jin deposed the prince Shensheng because he favored Li Ji, and replaced him with Xi Qi. This caused the Jin Dynasty to be in chaos for decades, and was ridiculed by the people of the world.
Because Qin Shihuang did not confirm Fusu as the crown prince early, he allowed Zhao Gao to use deception to establish Hu Hai, and ended up destroying himself. This is a fact that your Majesty has witnessed with your own eyes.
Now everyone in the world knows that the prince is benevolent, righteous, loyal and filial; Empress Lu and Your Majesty have gone through hardships and lived together, and they are a couple who share weal and woe. How can they turn their backs on her?
Your Majesty must abolish the eldest son and support the younger son. I would rather die first and let my blood stain the earth red.
Emperor Gao said: Forget it, sir, I was just talking casually.
Shusun Tong said: The prince is the foundation of the world. When the foundation is shaken, the world will shake. How can you use the foundation of the world as a joke?
Emperor Gao said: I listen to your opinion.
When the emperor set up a banquet to entertain the guests, he saw that the four senior scholars invited by Zhang Liang followed the prince into the palace to pay respects. The emperor had no idea of ​​replacing the prince anymore.
When Emperor Gao of the Han Dynasty passed away and Emperor Xiaohui came to the throne, he said to Mr. Shusun: None of the officials are familiar with the etiquette of the late emperor's cemetery and ancestral temple.
So he was appointed as Taichang and formulated the ceremonial regulations of the ancestral temple.
After that, many ceremonial systems of the Han Dynasty were successively formulated, all of which were recorded and recorded by Shusun Tong when he was appointed Taichang.
Emperor Xiaohui of the Han Dynasty wanted to go to the Changle Palace in the east to pay homage to the Empress Dowager Lu, and he often had small visits. Every time he went out, he had to clear the way. It was very annoying to block the passage, so he built a pavilion road just south of the arsenal of Weiyang Palace.
Shusun Tong reported to Emperor Xiaohui about his work, and took the opportunity to ask for a confidential talk with the emperor: How could your Majesty build a flyover without authorization over the passage from Gaobin to Gaomiao every month?
Gaodi Temple is the ancestral temple of the founder of the Han Dynasty. How can future generations be allowed to walk above the passage of the ancestral temple?
Emperor Xiaohui was horrified when he heard this and said: Destroy it quickly.
Mr. Shusun said: A monarch must not make wrong moves.
Now that it has been built, the people are all aware of it. If you want to destroy this overpass again, it will show that you have made a mistake.
I hope that your majesty will build another original ancestral temple to the north of the Wei River, send Emperor Gao's clothes there when he travels every month, and further expand and increase the ancestral temple. This is a fundamental measure of great filial piety.
The emperor then issued an edict to the relevant officials to build another ancestral temple.
This separate ancestral temple was built because of the pavilion road.
Emperor Xiaohui once traveled to the palace in the spring. Mr. Shusun said: In ancient times, there was a spring ritual of presenting cherries to the ancestral temple. Now is the season when cherries are ripe, so you can donate them. I hope that your majesty will pick some cherries to present to the ancestral temple when you travel.
The emperor agreed to do this.
Later, the ritual of offering various fruits flourished from this.
Tai Shigong said: Youdao is a fur coat worth a thousand gold, not the armpit skin of a fox; the rafters of a pavilion are not the branches of a tree; the achievements of the Xia, Shang and Zhou dynasties at that time are not the wisdom of a wise man.
Indeed!
Emperor Gaozu rose up from the humble common people, pacified the world, planned great plans, and fought with troops. It can be said that he did his best.
However, Liu Jing took off the crossbar of the cart and went to see the emperor for advice, which established a stable and great cause that will be passed down from generation to generation. How can talent and wisdom be exclusive to a few people?
Shusun Tong was good at watching the wind and steering the ship, weighing affairs, formulating etiquette and regulations, or adopting or discarding them. He was able to change with the times, and eventually became the master of Confucianism in the Han Dynasty. The most upright thing seems to be crooked, and the truth is always tortuous and forward. This is probably what we are talking about, right?
summary:
Liu Jing, a native of Qi, advised Emperor Gao of Han against establishing the capital in Luoyang, arguing that the Han Dynasty's rise differed from the Zhou Dynasty's gradual accumulation of virtue over generations. He noted that the Zhou built Luoyang as a central capital during their peak, but their decline showed that a weak situation, not just virtue, led to loss of control. In contrast, the Han emerged from warfare, with countless battles and suffering, making it unsuitable to emulate the Zhou. Liu Jing advocated for capitalizing in Guanzhong, protected by mountains and the Yellow River, with fertile land and a strategic position to "grab the world by its throat." Despite opposition from ministers favoring Luoyang, Zhang Liang's support led Emperor Gao to establish the capital in Guanzhong, rewarding Liu Jing with a surname change to Liu, the title of doctor, and the name Fengchun Jun.

Later, in the seventh year of Emperor Gao, Liu Jing was sent to scout the Xiongnu before a campaign against the rebellious King Xin of Han. He warned that the Xiongnu's display of weak soldiers and livestock was a ruse to ambush the Han army, advising against attack. Emperor Gao ignored this, imprisoned Liu Jing, and marched to Pingcheng, where he was besieged for seven days. After escaping, he released Liu Jing, rewarded him with 2,000 households, and granted him the title of Marquis of Guannei. To address Xiongnu threats, Liu Jing proposed a peace marriage alliance, suggesting Emperor Gao marry his eldest princess to Maodun Chanyu to ensure future submission through kinship. Emperor Gao agreed but, due to Empress Lu's protests, sent a palace maid instead, with Liu Jing negotiating the treaty.

Upon returning, Liu Jing warned that Xiongnu tribes near Chang'an posed a threat and recommended strengthening Guanzhong's population by moving powerful clans from Qi, Chu, and other states, as well as wealthy families, to bolster defenses against both the Xiongnu and potential rebellions. Emperor Gao approved, and Liu Jing relocated over 100,000 people.

Shusun Tong, a scholar from Xue County, served under Qin II, where he flattered the emperor by dismissing a rebellion as mere thievery, earning rewards but fleeing after Qin II punished those who spoke truthfully. He later served Xiang Liang, King Huai of Chu, and Xiang Yu before surrendering to Liu Bang, the King of Han. Disliking Confucian robes, Shusun Tong adopted Chu-style short coats, pleasing the king. He recommended warriors over his own disciples, explaining that the king needed fighters, not scholars, during wartime. After the Han unification, Emperor Gao abolished strict Qin rituals, leading to chaotic court behavior. Shusun Tong proposed creating a simplified court etiquette, blending ancient and Qin practices. He recruited 30 Confucian scholars, despite two refusing, and practiced for a month. In the seventh year, the Changle Palace ceremony impressed Emperor Gao, who remarked, "Only today do I know the dignity of being an emperor." Shusun Tong was made Taichang, rewarded with gold, and his disciples received official posts.

As crown prince tutor in the ninth year, Shusun Tong opposed Emperor Gao's plan to replace Crown Prince Liu Ying with Zhao Wang Liu Ruyi, citing historical disasters from such actions. He emphasized the prince's virtue and Empress Lu's shared hardships, threatening death if the change proceeded. Emperor Gao relented, especially after seeing four scholars supporting the prince. Under Emperor Xiaohui, Shusun Tong served as Taichang, formulating ancestral temple rituals. He advised against a pavilion road built over the Gaodi Temple passage, suggesting a new temple instead, and introduced cherry offerings to the temple. Sima Qian concluded that Liu Jing's strategic advice and Shusun Tong's adaptable ritual-making were crucial to the Han's stability, highlighting their unique contributions.
